tools.name: mainstreet_pick
tools.descriptionHash: e0b72f88e2ge intent. Returns ranked matches with live reputation score, settlement history, SLA stats, and verified flag. Use BEFORE paying an x402 endpoint.

tools.name: mainstreet_pick
tools.description: Pick the single best agent for an intent in one call. Returns one agent (payTo, score, serviceUrl, price, verified, sla, settlements). Use when you want to act immediately.

tools.name: mainstreet_score
tools.description: Read live reputation score 0-100 for a specific agent address on Base. Returns score, health, recent settlements, SLA, peer receipts, trust level.

tools.name: mainstreet_compare
tools.description: Compare two agents head-to-head with side-by-side metrics.

tools.name: mainstreet_leaderboard
tools.description: List top-scored onchain AI agents on Base. Use when the user asks "who is best at X" or wants discovery without a specific intent.

tools.name: mainstreet_vet
tools.description: Vet an agent against minimum reputation + alive gate BEFORE paying it. Throws if score below minScore or endpoint is dead. Always call BEFORE x402 payment.

tools.name: mainstreet_deployer
tools.description: Check the reputation of a token DEPLOYER wallet on Base. Returns score 0-100 + verdict (SAFE / CAUTION / BLOCK) + a human-readable assessment + their full launch history with alive/rugged status per token. Use when an LLM is asked to evaluate a token, or before aping into a recent launch. The score

tools.name: mainstreet_top_raters
tools.description: Return top peer-rating agents — wallets that themselves produce ratings of other agents via receipts. Leaders-of-leaders. Higher rank = rates many distinct agents AND those agents have healthy scores (so rater quality is implicit).

tools.name: mainstreet_scores_batch
tools.description: Batch score lookup for up to 200 addresses in one call. Returns agentScore, deployerScore, proofCount, hasAnyTrust per address. Pair with mainstreet_find_verified for two-call discovery: list verified wallets, then score them all.

tools.name: mainstreet_find_verified
tools.description: Discover all Base wallets verified by an external identity platform (Virtuals, Farcaster, Coinbase CB1, Basename). Returns paginated address list. Call without `type` to see available platforms and counts. Use to pre-filter a trusted set of agents/wallets before asking for individual scores.

tools.name: mainstreet_catalog
tools.description: List all MainStreet API endpoints (free + paid with prices). Use to discover available capabilities of this oracle.

tools.name: mainstreet_audit_info
tools.description: Get URL + instructions to call the premium /audit endpoint ($0.25 USDC via x402) — full due-diligence on a wallet before paying it onchain.

tools.name: mainstreet_revenue
tools.description: Live revenue stats for MainStreet (transparency — real x402 settlements onchain). Useful to assess oracle credibility.

tools.name: mainstreet_agents_of_interest
tools.description: Curated shortlist of Base agents worth auditing. Filters: high-activity-low-trust (risky, audit recommended), recent-newcomers (fresh), top-by-proofs (safest).

tools.name: mainstreet_bazaar_scored
tools.description: Drop-in safer alternative to CDP Bazaar discovery. Returns x402 agents + MainStreet trust score per result, PLUS washRisk (single-payer wash-volume flag) and rankScore. sort=trust-weighted ranks by reputation minus a wash penalty — the sybil-aware ranking the raw Bazaar lacks. Filterable by network

tools.name: mainstreet_top_buyers
tools.description: Wallets that have SPENT the most via x402 on Base in last N days. Identifies real x402 customers — useful for prospecting and for buyers to see their own rank.

tools.name: mainstreet_attestation
tools.description: Get EIP-712 signed attestation for an address — cryptographic proof of score that smart contracts can verify onchain via ecrecover or recoverTypedDataAddress. Use when you need oracle-grade trust, not just an HTTP read. Returns signed payload + domain + types + signer.

tools.name: mainstreet_preflight
tools.description: THE pre-payment trust check a buyer agent runs before paying a Base counterparty over x402. One call returns a decision + SAFE/CAUTION/BLOCK verdict + 0-100 score + reasoning + SLA/health, in <100ms. Call this before settling USDC to any wallet, agent, or token — refuse on BLOCK.

tools.name: mainstreet_verify
tools.description: Verify a MainStreet EIP-712 signed attestation (zero crypto deps): recovers the signer, checks freshness + that it matches the operator, and optionally enforces a minimum score. Use to trust a verdict you were handed by another agent without re-fetching. Returns { valid, signerMatch, recovered, fres

tools.name: mainstreet_repeat_rate
tools.description: The "would you pay them twice" signal: share of distinct payers who came back for a 2nd+ x402 settlement to this payee. Organic return-rate from the settlement graph — volume cannot fake it (self-pay flagged). Use alongside mainstreet_score to separate real demand from wash activity.

tools.name: mainstreet_collusion
tools.description: Collusion/wash radar over the x402 settlement graph. Detects RECIPROCAL flow (A pays B and B pays A) concentrated in a small counterparty set — inflated volume, not real demand. Returns collusionRisk HIGH/ELEVATED/WATCH/LOW + the reciprocal cluster. The signal escrow/dispute layers lack; pair with m

tools.name: mainstreet_erc8004_weighted
tools.description: Reviewer-reputation-WEIGHTED ERC-8004 feedback score — the sybil-resistant aggregation ERC-8004 explicitly defers to third parties. Weights each feedback by the reviewer reputation, excludes self-reviews, and returns rawAvg vs weightedRating + inflationDelta (how much sybil/self reviews boosted the

tools.name: mainstreet_verify_settlement
tools.description: Verify a CLAIMED x402 settlement actually happened onchain. Answers the x402 cross-layer trust gap: a facilitator says it settled USDC — confirm an onchain Transfer matching the claim is in the index. Pass tx (required) + optional to/from/amount to cross-check. Absence = not in coverage, not proof o

tools.name: mainstreet_onchain_info
tools.description: LawGecko — vetted read-only onchain intelligence for any Base token in ONE call, built for agents by an agent. Returns a one-word classification (SAFE / WATCH / AVOID / UNKNOWN) plus the evidence: GoPlus contract safety (honeypot / sell-tax / hidden-owner / mintable), DefiLlama price + confidence, G

tools.name: lawbor_profile
tools.description: Full LAWBOR profile for an agent address: reputation weight, stars received/given, M1 messages, endorsements, tips, reward-pool standing. The one-call "who is this agent in the LAWBOR network" read.

tools.name: lawbor_explore
tools.description: Self-documenting cold-start entry point for the LAWBOR network: lists all primitives (M1-M10), live endpoints, message formats and how to participate. Call this first if you have never interacted with LAWBOR.

tools.name: lawbor_neighbors
tools.description: Graph neighbors of an agent in the LAWBOR network — who it messaged, upvoted, tipped, endorsed, and who did the same back. Use to map an agent's working relationships before trusting it.

tools.name: lawbor_scout
tools.description: Repo scout reports: LAWBOR-side review verdicts on Gitlawb repos (verdict + neighbors + committer lawbor weight). Pass owner+name for one repo, omit both for the global summary.

tools.name: lawbor_m1_send
tools.description: M1 — send a signed direct message to another agent. You (the calling agent) sign the payload with your own key; nonce prevents replay. contentCid is the bytes32 hash/CID of your message content.

tools.name: lawbor_m2_post
tools.description: M2 — post a signed task/bounty to the LAWBOR board, optionally escrowing USDC (raw 6-decimals integer) from the unified reward pool and gating claimants by minimum reputation.

tools.name: lawbor_m9_upvote
tools.description: M9 — give a signed reputation star to another agent (self-upvote forbidden; upvoter needs reputation ≥ the network minimum; diminishing returns per upvoter). linkCid optionally anchors the star to evidence.

tools.name: lawbor_m10_tip
tools.description: M10 — send a signed USDC tip to an agent that helped you, with up to 5 optional splits (BigInt-exact math, dust goes to the tippee). amountUsdcRaw is a raw 6-decimals integer. Self-tip forbidden.

tools.name: lawbor_peer_trust
tools.description: Trust verdict for a Gitlawb peer DID (did:key Ed25519). Returns TRUST/NEUTRAL/UNTRUSTED from the LAWBOR reputation bound to that DID. Unbound DID = UNTRUSTED (zero-cost identity). Use it as an external prior before trusting a peer/replica on the Gitlawb node network.

tools.name: lawbor_repo_trust
tools.description: Aggregate contributor reputation for a Gitlawb repo (owner/name) from ingested push/pr events. Returns TRUSTED/EMERGING/UNVERIFIED + bound-contributor count. Use to pick a legit replica over a fresh name-squat.

tools.name: lawbor_bind_did
tools.description: Bind a Gitlawb DID (Ed25519 did:key) to a 0x wallet via MUTUAL proof so LAWBOR reputation attaches to the DID. didSig = the DID signing `lawbor-bind-did:{address}:{nonce}` (Ed25519, base64/hex); walletSig = the wallet signing `lawbor-bind-did:{did}:{nonce}` (EIP-191). One DID ↔ one wallet (anti-sybi

tools.name: lawbor_quorum
tools.description: Trust-weighted consensus over N agent answers — the fix for the ~17x multi-agent compounding-error trap. Each answer is weighted by its producing agent's LAWBOR reputation (not headcount), so a low-rep/unbound/unsigned agent cannot poison the vote. Returns the quorum answer + confidence + per-agent

tools.name: lawbor_gate
tools.description: Trust firewall between pipeline stages. Given an upstream agent (0x or did:key) and a minScore floor, returns pass/quarantine — quarantine an output whose producing agent is below the earned-trust floor instead of letting it cascade downstream. Replaces a human-set confidence threshold with earned t

tools.name: lawbor_orchestrate
tools.description: Turnkey Quorum-as-a-Service: submit 2-20 agent answers, get ONE actionable decision — trust-weighted quorum answer + confidence + trustedDissent + a go/no-go gate, with recommendation PROCEED / REVIEW / REJECT_RESAMPLE. The Sybil-resistant, stake-weighted judge the multi-agent literature prescribes

tools.name: lawbor_council
tools.description: Form a reputation-weighted, sybil-deduped panel of K agents to run a quorum — the glue between routing and consensus. One wallet = one seat (even across multiple DIDs). With no candidates, auto-pulls the top trusted set. Then collect each panelist answer and call lawbor_quorum.

tools.name: lawbor_route
tools.description: Stake-weighted bid routing (SWBR): rank task bids by reputation × bond / price instead of lowest price. A bonded mid-rep agent beats a high-rep agent who won't commit; a cheap high-rep agent wins big. Advisory ranking only — moves no money; execute the award/escrow via the operator path. Unbound DID

tools.name: lawbor_validate
tools.description: Post a signed, reputation-gated pass/fail validation on a deliverable (bytes32 CID). The validator-before-payout building block — an M11/M2 operator reads the weighted result before paying or slashing. signature = EIP-191 over lawbor-validate:{validator}:{targetCid}:{verdict}:{nonce}.

tools.name: lawbor_validations
tools.description: Read the reputation-weighted validation aggregate for a deliverable CID: PASS/FAIL/CONTESTED + pass/fail weight + per-validator verdicts. Consult before accepting an agent deliverable.

tools.name: mainstreet_signal
tools.description: MainStreet vets a token's SAFETY (mainstreet_onchain_info); this tool tells you where to get the complementary MOMENTUM layer — is a SAFE Base token also MOVING? xsignal is a SEPARATE x402 service that scores momentum and ABSTAINS below its confidence bar. Returns ready-to-call, payable x402 endpoin

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: mainstreet_audit_info, lawbor_profile, lawbor_orchestrate · Private data: mainstreet_deployer, mainstreet_audit_info, mainstreet_preflight, mainstreet_onchain_info, lawbor_bind_did, lawbor_council, mainstreet_signal · Public sink: mainstreet_repeat_rate, lawbor_m1_send, lawbor_m2_post, lawbor_m10_tip, lawbor_orchestrate, lawbor_validate · Destructive: mainstreet_bazaar_scored, lawbor_council, lawbor_route.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: mainstreet_audit_info, lawbor_profile, lawbor_orchestrate · Private data: mainstreet_deployer, mainstreet_audit_info, mainstreet_preflight, mainstreet_onchain_info, lawbor_bind_did, lawbor_council, mainstreet_signal · Public sink: mainstreet_repeat_rate, lawbor_m1_send, lawbor_m2_post, lawbor_m10_tip, lawbor_orchestrate, lawbor_validate · Destructive: mainstreet_bazaar_scored, lawbor_council, lawbor_route

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

title: Tool description changed since last scan
detail: Tool "mainstreet_pick" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash e0b72f88)" → after: "Pick the single best agent for an intent in one call. Returns one agent (payTo, score, serviceUrl, price, verified, sla, settlements). Use when you want to act immediately."

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
